message:
chore(beads): Close completed tasks from UX Polish sprint

Closed:
- phage_explorer-l5vo: Remove SQLite message
- phage_explorer-de4o: Fix zoom-to-extents
- phage_explorer-qm08: CPK coloring
- phage_explorer-elbv: AA rename (already done)
- phage_explorer-xkxf: Remove Cartoon mode
- phage_explorer-z49z: Verify phages

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -207,7 +207,7 @@ {"id":"phage_explorer-eb51","title":"[WEB-P3] Add z-index management and sorting","description":"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:02:25.336008-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:27:08.459382-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:27:08.459382-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-eb51","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-zld6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:07:36.698121-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-ee1","title":"[WEB-P3] Overlays & Panels: UI Component System","description":"# Phase 3: Overlays & Panels (Target: Weeks 5-7) ## Purpose Build the interactive UI layer that sits atop the visualization. Command palette, analysis menus, overlays, and help systems. ## Why This Matters The keyboard-first design requires a sophisticated command palette and hotkey system. Analysis overlays are how users extract insights from sequences. Without this layer, the app is just a viewer, not an explorer. ## Design Principles 1. **Every action has a hotkey** - No mouse-required features 2. **Fuzzy search everywhere** - Command palette, search, menus 3. **Modal consistency** - All panels behave the same way 4. **Layer stacking** - Multiple overlays can be active ## Deliverables 1. **Command Palette** - Fuzzy search, keyboard navigation, `:` trigger 2. **Analysis Menu** - Drawer with all analysis options 3. **Simulation Hub** - Modal launcher for simulations 4. **Layer 1 Overlays** - GC, complexity, bendability, promoter, repeat 5. **Multi-Overlay Manager** - Stack management, z-index, focus trap 6. **Help Overlay** - Dynamic hotkey reference ## Component Architecture ### Command Palette ``` Features: - Fuzzy search with highlighting - Category grouping (theme:, overlay:, sim:, export:) - Recent commands - Keyboard navigation (↑↓, Enter, Tab complete) - Cyberpunk styling (glow, scanlines) ``` ### Overlay Stack Manager ```typescript class OverlayStackManager { push(overlay): void // Add overlay to stack pop(id?): void // Remove overlay toggle(overlay): void // Toggle overlay visibility getTopmost(): Overlay // For focus management hasBlockingModal(): bool // Prevent background interaction } ``` ### Panel System ``` Positions: left, right, bottom, center Sizes: sm, md, lg, xl, full Features: - Focus trapping - Escape to close - Spring animations - Scanline overlay decoration ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All hotkeys from TUI work in web app - [ ] Command palette with fuzzy search - [ ] Overlays toggle correctly with hotkeys - [ ] Multiple overlays can stack visually - [ ] Panel animations are smooth (60fps) - [ ] Help overlay shows all current hotkeys - [ ] Focus trap works for modals ## Hotkey Parity Checklist ``` Navigation: ↑↓←→, hjkl, gg, G, Ctrl+g View: Space, 1/2/3, f, t, T, d, m, M, z Overlays: g, x, b, p, r, J, L, V, Y, 0 Panels: a/A, S, :, w/W, /, s, ?, Ctrl+p ``` ## Risks & Mitigations - **Risk**: Fuzzy search performance with many commands - **Mitigation**: Use efficient algorithm (fzy/fzf-style) - **Risk**: Focus management complexity - **Mitigation**: Use React focus trap library, test thoroughly - **Risk**: Animation performance - **Mitigation**: Use CSS transforms, avoid layout thrashing ## Dependencies - Phase 0 (keyboard manager) - Phase 2 (core visualization to overlay on)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:56:15.528715-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:09:35.227829-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:09:35.227829-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ee1","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-87z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:04:32.337246-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ee1","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-10p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:04:33.281302-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-eju4","title":"Web App TUI Feature Parity","description":"## Vision Transform the web application from a static landing page into a **complete replica of the TUI** with all features, but with **superior visualizations** using Canvas/WebGL instead of ASCII art. ## Background & Rationale The TUI (Terminal User Interface) is a sophisticated genome exploration application with: - **31 overlay types** for analysis, visualization, and navigation - **6 simulation engines** (lysogeny, ribosome traffic, plaque formation, evolution, infection kinetics, packaging motor) - **Vim-style keyboard navigation** with modal modes (NORMAL, SEARCH, COMMAND) - **Progressive disclosure** (novice → intermediate → power user tiers) The web package already has excellent infrastructure (~60+ components/utilities), but the main App.tsx was simplified to a static landing page. This epic restores and enhances the full functionality. ## Why This Matters 1. **Accessibility**: Not everyone has a terminal environment or wants to install a CLI tool 2. **Discoverability**: A web app is shareable via URL and searchable 3. **Visual Excellence**: Canvas/WebGL enables visualizations impossible in ASCII 4. **Cross-Platform**: Works on any device with a browser ## Success Criteria - [ ] All 31 overlay types from TUI are available - [ ] All keyboard shortcuts work identically - [ ] All 6 simulations are runnable - [ ] Genome comparison fully functional - [ ] 3D model viewing available - [ ] Performance meets targets (60fps scrolling, <3s TTI) - [ ] Works offline after initial load ## Technical Approach - Use `sql.js` for SQLite in browser (WebPhageRepository) - Leverage existing `@phage-explorer/state` Zustand store - Web Workers for analysis computation (ComputeOrchestrator exists) - Canvas-based rendering for all visualizations - WebGL/Three.js for 3D protein structures ## Reference See: PLAN_TO_MAKE_WEB_APP_MIRROR_ALL_FUNCTIONALITY_FROM_TUI.md","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:52:46.049548-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T17:41:19.162738-05:00"} -{"id":"phage_explorer-elbv","title":"Rename AA to Amino Acids in UI","description":"","notes":"# Rename "AA" to "Amino Acids" in UI ## Problem Statement The abbreviation "AA" is used throughout the web app's UI to refer to "Amino Acids." This creates confusion for non-specialists who may not know this abbreviation. Potential misinterpretations of "AA": - "Alcoholics Anonymous" - "Associate in Arts" (degree) - "American Airlines" - "Automobile Association" - "Anti-Aircraft" ## Current Occurrences ### SequenceView.tsx (lines 148, 262-273) ```tsx const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'AA' : 'Dual'; // ^^^^ ``` ### Store/Types ```typescript type ViewMode = 'dna' | 'aa' | 'dual'; // ^^^^ ``` ## Solution Replace all user-facing instances of "AA" with "Amino Acids" or context-appropriate full text: ### UI Labels - "AA" → "Amino Acids" - "DNA/AA view" → "DNA/Amino Acids view" - "AA mode" → "Amino Acid mode" ### Button Text - Short form: "Amino Acids" (fits in most buttons) - If space-constrained: "Amino" (still clearer than "AA") ### Accessibility Labels ```tsx aria-label="Toggle between DNA and Amino Acid view" ``` ## Implementation ### SequenceView.tsx Changes ```tsx // Before const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'AA' : 'Dual'; // After const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'Amino Acids' : 'Dual'; ``` ### Footer Hints ```tsx // Before v view // After v toggle DNA/Amino Acids ``` ### Screen Reader Description ```tsx // Before Sequence view in {viewModeLabel} mode // After Sequence view showing {viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA nucleotide' : 'amino acid'} sequence ``` ## Files to Modify 1. `/packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx` - Line 148: Change viewModeLabel - Lines 359-367: Update footer hints - Lines 371-375: Update screen reader text 2. Search for other occurrences: ```bash grep -r '"AA"' packages/web/src/ grep -r "'AA'" packages/web/src/ grep -r "AA view" packages/web/src/ ``` ## Notes - The internal type `ViewMode = 'dna' | 'aa' | 'dual'` can remain unchanged (developers know this) - Only user-facing strings need to change - Consider adding a glossary/tooltip for first-time users ## Testing - [ ] All "AA" references in UI replaced - [ ] Screen reader announces "Amino Acids" not "AA" - [ ] Button text fits without overflow - [ ] Consistent terminology throughout app ## Definition of Done - [ ] No user-facing "AA" abbreviations remain - [ ] "Amino Acids" spelled out in all UI text - [ ] Accessibility labels updated - [ ] Footer hints updated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:52.720252-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:41.935176-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:41.935176-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-elbv","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-8mgm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.091769-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"phage_explorer-elbv","title":"Rename AA to Amino Acids in UI","description":"","notes":"# Rename "AA" to "Amino Acids" in UI ## Problem Statement The abbreviation "AA" is used throughout the web app's UI to refer to "Amino Acids." This creates confusion for non-specialists who may not know this abbreviation. Potential misinterpretations of "AA": - "Alcoholics Anonymous" - "Associate in Arts" (degree) - "American Airlines" - "Automobile Association" - "Anti-Aircraft" ## Current Occurrences ### SequenceView.tsx (lines 148, 262-273) ```tsx const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'AA' : 'Dual'; // ^^^^ ``` ### Store/Types ```typescript type ViewMode = 'dna' | 'aa' | 'dual'; // ^^^^ ``` ## Solution Replace all user-facing instances of "AA" with "Amino Acids" or context-appropriate full text: ### UI Labels - "AA" → "Amino Acids" - "DNA/AA view" → "DNA/Amino Acids view" - "AA mode" → "Amino Acid mode" ### Button Text - Short form: "Amino Acids" (fits in most buttons) - If space-constrained: "Amino" (still clearer than "AA") ### Accessibility Labels ```tsx aria-label="Toggle between DNA and Amino Acid view" ``` ## Implementation ### SequenceView.tsx Changes ```tsx // Before const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'AA' : 'Dual'; // After const viewModeLabel = viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA' : viewMode === 'aa' ? 'Amino Acids' : 'Dual'; ``` ### Footer Hints ```tsx // Before v view // After v toggle DNA/Amino Acids ``` ### Screen Reader Description ```tsx // Before Sequence view in {viewModeLabel} mode // After Sequence view showing {viewMode === 'dna' ? 'DNA nucleotide' : 'amino acid'} sequence ``` ## Files to Modify 1. `/packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx` - Line 148: Change viewModeLabel - Lines 359-367: Update footer hints - Lines 371-375: Update screen reader text 2. Search for other occurrences: ```bash grep -r '"AA"' packages/web/src/ grep -r "'AA'" packages/web/src/ grep -r "AA view" packages/web/src/ ``` ## Notes - The internal type `ViewMode = 'dna' | 'aa' | 'dual'` can remain unchanged (developers know this) - Only user-facing strings need to change - Consider adding a glossary/tooltip for first-time users ## Testing - [ ] All "AA" references in UI replaced - [ ] Screen reader announces "Amino Acids" not "AA" - [ ] Button text fits without overflow - [ ] Consistent terminology throughout app ## Definition of Done - [ ] No user-facing "AA" abbreviations remain - [ ] "Amino Acids" spelled out in all UI text - [ ] Accessibility labels updated - [ ] Footer hints updated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:52.720252-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:44:26.035538-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-09T11:44:26.035538-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-elbv","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-8mgm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.091769-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-enf","title":"[WEB-P2] Pre-render amino acid characters (20+stop)","description":"","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:14.782042-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:01:23.068058-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:01:23.068058-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-enf","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-53f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:05:05.86519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-eob","title":"[WEB-P0] Setup React 19 with concurrent features","description":"Configure React 19 with concurrent rendering, transitions, and automatic batching","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:10.497345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:20:34.890412-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T17:20:34.890412-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-eob","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-94x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:04:37.122052-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-ew9a","title":"Phase 7: Polish & Optimization","description":"## Overview Final polish phase focusing on performance optimization, accessibility, progressive disclosure, and overall user experience refinement. ## Why This Phase After core features are implemented, users will discover: - Performance bottlenecks with large genomes - Accessibility gaps for keyboard-only users - Feature overwhelm for new users - Edge cases and rough edges This phase addresses these real-world usage issues. ## Technical Components ### 1. Performance Optimization **Virtual Scrolling**: - Ensure PhageList handles 10,000+ phages smoothly - Optimize SequenceGrid for megabase genomes - Minimize re-renders with proper memoization **Memory Management**: - Unload analysis results when overlays close - Clear canvas buffers when views change - Implement LRU cache for computed data **Worker Load Balancing**: - Multiple workers for parallel analysis - Priority queue for user-visible computations - Cancel stale requests when user navigates **Lazy Loading**: - Code-split overlay components - Load simulation workers on-demand - Progressive database loading (index first, sequences on-demand) ### 2. Accessibility **Keyboard Navigation**: - Full app usable without mouse - Focus management in overlays - Tab order makes sense - Escape closes overlays **Screen Reader Support**: - ARIA labels on interactive elements - Announce analysis results - Describe visualizations textually **Reduced Motion**: - Respect prefers-reduced-motion - Disable animations for sensitive users - Static alternatives for dynamic content **Color Accessibility**: - Ensure sufficient contrast - Don't rely on color alone - Colorblind-friendly palettes ### 3. Progressive Disclosure **User Tiers**: ``` Novice (0-5 sessions): - Show basic overlays only - Prominent help hints - Simplified keyboard shortcuts Intermediate (5-20 sessions): - Unlock more overlays - Show keyboard hints on hover - Introduce simulations Power User (20+ sessions): - All features available - Minimal hints - Full keyboard shortcut set ``` **Tier Detection**: - Track session count in localStorage - Manual tier selection in settings - Reset option **Feature Gating**: - Dim unavailable overlays in menu - Tooltip explains how to unlock - Celebration on tier promotion ### 4. Error Handling **Graceful Degradation**: - Fallback if WebGL unavailable - Handle failed database loads - Recover from worker crashes **User Feedback**: - Clear error messages (not technical jargon) - Suggested actions for common errors - Report issue link ### 5. Testing & Validation **Performance Metrics**: | Metric | Target | |--------|--------| | First Contentful Paint | < 1.5s | | Time to Interactive | < 3s | | Database Load | < 5s | | Sequence Scroll | 60fps | | Analysis Compute | < 2s | **Test Coverage**: - Unit tests for analysis functions - Component tests for overlays - Integration tests for user flows - E2E tests with Playwright ## Dependencies - **Depends on**: All previous phases (this is polish) - **Blocks**: None (final phase) ## Success Criteria - [ ] App loads in < 3 seconds - [ ] Sequence scrolls at 60fps - [ ] All features keyboard-accessible - [ ] WCAG 2.1 AA compliance - [ ] Progressive disclosure implemented - [ ] Error states handled gracefully ## Files to Modify ``` packages/web/src/ ├── hooks/useAccessibility.ts # NEW ├── hooks/useProgressiveDisclosure.ts # NEW ├── components/**/*.tsx # Add ARIA, optimize renders ├── styles/ # Accessibility improvements └── utils/performance.ts # NEW ``` ## Estimated Complexity: Medium This phase is distributed across the codebase rather than concentrated in new files. It requires attention to detail more than new architecture.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:05.727066-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:05.727066-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ew9a","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-bjoz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:23.042703-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ew9a","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-5a7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:23.136195-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ew9a","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-upat","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:23.22536-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ew9a","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-mkpk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:23.303367-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
